verses.text: For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.
I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding.
For I know the plans I have for you, plans to prosper you and not to harm you.
Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid, for the Lord will be with you.
The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.

livePrayer.title: Live Prayer
livePrayer.noPastors: No pastors praying live at the moment.
livePrayer.explainerDesc: Here you can participate in real-time prayer moments with pastors from various churches. When a pastor starts a prayer session, you can follow along, pray together, and feel God's presence with brothers and sisters worldwide.
livePrayer.explainerVerse: "For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them." — Matthew 18:20

churchRegister.title: Register Church
churchRegister.subtitle: Register your church on the Sigo com Fé platform
churchRegister.denominationPlaceholder: E.g.: Baptist, Assembly of God, etc.
churchRegister.loginRequired: You need to be logged in to register a church.
churchRegister.heroSubtitle: Connect your community to a global faith network and transform how you pastor
churchRegister.mainVerse: 📖 "They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer." — Acts 2:42
churchRegister.benefit1Desc: Your church appears to thousands of Christians worldwide looking for a faith community.
churchRegister.benefit2Desc: As a pastor, you get access to an exclusive area with professional tools to manage your entire church digitally.
churchRegister.benefit3Desc: Register your church and start using essential tools at no cost. Our mission is to serve the Kingdom of God!
churchRegister.pastorVerse: "Be shepherds of God's flock that is under your care, watching over them..." — 1 Peter 5:2
churchRegister.availableInRoom: 🔑 All this is available in the Pastor Management Room
churchRegister.quickInfo: ⚡ Quick and easy — takes less than 2 minutes
churchRegister.featMembersDesc: See who is connected, track attendance, and strengthen your digital community.
churchRegister.featTithesDesc: Complete financial control: record tithes, offerings, and generate transparent reports.
churchRegister.featPrayersDesc: Receive and track prayer requests from members. Pray for them and mark when God answers!
churchRegister.featChatDesc: Chat in real-time with those who need guidance. Automatic translation to any language!
churchRegister.featAnnounceDesc: Send notices of services, special events, and prayer campaigns to the entire congregation.
churchRegister.featAgendaDesc: Organize services, small groups, rehearsals, leadership meetings, and events on a calendar.
churchRegister.featStudiesDesc: Create interactive studies and Bible reading plans for your congregation to grow in the Word.
churchRegister.featReportsDesc: Statistics on growth, attendance, engagement, and finances of your church.
churchRegister.featConsecrationDesc: Organize fasts and consecrations with your church. Track who is fasting in real-time!

pastorChat.title: Pastoral Chat
pastorChat.subtitle: Chat directly with a pastor. Automatic translation included.
pastorChat.waitingDesc: A pastor will join the conversation soon. Have faith!
pastorChat.noWaiting: No one waiting at the moment.
pastorChat.introTitle: 🕊️ A Pastor Is Ready to Listen to You
pastorChat.introDesc: Sometimes life brings burdens too heavy to carry alone. Here you can have a confidential conversation with a pastor who will listen to you, advise you with biblical wisdom, and pray for you.
pastorChat.introVerse1: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. — Matthew 11:28
pastorChat.introFeatures:
💜 Confidential and secure conversation
🌍 Automatic translation in 15 languages
🙏 Volunteer pastors from around the world
pastorChat.introVerse2: Plans fail for lack of counsel, but with many advisers they succeed. — Proverbs 15:22

friends.title: Friends
friends.noFriends: You don't have any friends yet. Search for people to connect!

helpRequests.title: 🆘 How Do Help Requests Work?
helpRequests.subtitle: People in need of spiritual support and prayer. Respond with love.
helpRequests.howWorks: This is a safe and confidential space where believers in difficulty can share their challenges and receive pastoral support. Each request is an opportunity to demonstrate the love of Christ.
helpRequests.statusPending: 🔴 Pending — Waiting for a pastor to attend
helpRequests.statusInProgress: 🟡 In Progress — A pastor is already caring
helpRequests.statusResolved: 🟢 Resolved — The person received the necessary support
helpRequests.verse: Levai as cargas uns dos outros e assim cumprireis a lei de Cristo. — Gálatas 6:2
helpRequests.empty: No help requests at the moment. Relief!

profile.addPhotoDesc: Tap here to personalize your profile with a photo.

messages.title: Messages
messages.whenSomeone: When someone sends you a message, it will appear here!

groups.title: Groups
groups.communionDesc: Groups are spaces to share, learn, and grow together in faith. Create or join Bible study, prayer, worship, or any group that unites brothers and sisters in Christ.
groups.communionVerse: 📖 "Not giving up meeting together, as some are in the habit of doing, but encouraging one another." — Hebrews 10:25

consecration.title: Consecration
consecration.subtitle: Fasting and prayer - strengthen your faith
consecration.noOneYet: No one consecrating yet. Be the first!
consecration.conclusion: Fasting: It's not a diet. It's not an empty sacrifice. It's obedience, alignment and dependence on God.
consecration.powerDesc: Fasting is one of the most powerful spiritual weapons God has given us. It's not just abstaining from food — it's surrendering entirely to God, seeking His presence and His will above all else.
consecration.beginFastingDesc: Tap the flame button below to indicate that you are starting your consecration (fasting and prayer).
consecration.flameRisesDesc: While you are consecrating, a flame of fire rises on the screen representing you. Each person is a flame!
consecration.endFastingDesc: When finished, tap the button again to end. Your flame goes out, but your prayer remains!
consecration.morePeople: The more people consecrating, the more flames of fire rise! Each flame is a person in prayer.
consecration.currentlyConsecrating: You are consecrating now! Tap the button when you finish.
consecration.touchButton: Tap the button above to START your consecration
consecration.activeFastingDesc: each person = a flame of fire 🔥
consecration.spiritualStrength: Strengthens the spirit and subdues the flesh
consecration.mentalHumility: Humbling of the soul and inner peace
consecration.breakGlory: Everything for the glory of God

common.reported: Post reported! Moderators will review it.

reflection.title: Reflection with God
reflection.subtitle: Take a moment. Breathe. Listen to God's voice.
reflection.placeholder: Write here what God spoke to your heart...
reflection.saved: Reflection saved in God's heart! (Soon we will save it to your profile)
questions: What did you do today that gladdened God's heart?
Is there someone you need to forgive today?
Have you put God first or the worries of the world?
If Jesus returned now, how would He find your heart?
When was the last time you heard God's voice?
Did your words today build up or hurt someone?
What can you be thankful for today, even amidst difficulties?
Did you trust God or try to solve everything with your own strength?
Who needs your prayer today?
Have you been a reflection of Christ's love to others?
What is stopping you from fully surrendering to God?
Did you read the Bible today seeking to know God or just out of obligation?
How can you serve someone today without expecting anything in return?
Where did you see God's hand in your day?
Have you harbored resentment in your heart?
If God asked you to do something difficult today, would you obey?
Have you been grateful for your daily bread?
Has your life inspired others to seek God?
Did you set aside quality time to talk to the Father today?
What do you need to leave behind to move forward with God?
Have you loved your neighbor as yourself?
Is your faith based on feelings or on God's Word?
Have you been faithful in the little God entrusted to you?
What would you do differently today if you knew it was your last day?
Have you asked God for wisdom before making decisions?
Is your heart at peace or anxious? Surrender it to Him.
Have you taken care of the temple of the Holy Spirit (your body)?
Have you shared the good news of the Gospel?
What has God been trying to teach you in this season?
Are you willing to forgive as Christ forgave you?

music.verse: "Sing to the Lord a new song; sing to the Lord, all the earth." — Psalm 96:1

courseFinance.title: Biblical Finance
courseFinance.subtitle: Learn to manage your finances according to God's principles
courseFinance.securePayment: Secure payment via Stripe • Immediate access
courseFinance.pastorOnly: Some lessons are restricted to pastors only.

l1.title: God's Purpose for Money
l1.desc: Discover what the Bible really teaches about wealth and poverty.

l2.title: Tithing: Commandment or Principle?
l2.desc: A deep analysis of tithing in the Old and New Testaments.

l3.title: Offerings and Generosity
l3.desc: How to give cheerfully and the principle of sowing and reaping.

l4.title: Freedom from Debt
l4.desc: Biblical strategies to get out of debt and live free.

l5.title: Saving: The Wisdom of the Ant
l5.desc: Proverbs teaches about saving. Learn to build reserves.

l6.title: Biblical Family Budget
l6.desc: How to organize your finances with stewardship principles.

l7.title: Investing with Wisdom
l7.desc: Parable of the Talents: multiply what God has given you.

l8.title: Contentment vs. Greed
l8.desc: Financial peace starts in the heart, not the bank account.

l9.title: Work as Worship
l9.desc: Colossians 3:23 — working for the Lord, not for men.

l10.title: Prosperity with Purpose
l10.desc: God prospers so you can be a blessing, not to hoard.

l11.title: Marriage and Finances
l11.desc: How to align finances in marriage with biblical principles.

l12.title: Teaching Kids about Money
l12.desc: Raise financially wise children with a biblical foundation.

l13.title: Christian Entrepreneurship
l13.desc: Businesses that honor God and serve others.

l14.title: Planning for the Future
l14.desc: Retirement, inheritance, and biblical financial legacy.

l15.title: Total Stewardship
l15.desc: Everything belongs to God — you are a manager, not an owner.

courseTheology.title: Christian Theology
courseTheology.subtitle: Deepen your theological knowledge and strengthen your faith with foundation
courseTheology.securePayment: Secure payment via Stripe • Immediate access
courseTheology.emailPrompt: Your email to receive the course:

l1.title: Introduction to Theology
l1.desc: What theology is and why every Christian should study it.

l2.title: Revelation of God
l2.desc: General and special revelation: how God reveals Himself to man.

l3.title: Bibliology
l3.desc: The doctrine of Scripture: inspiration, inerrancy, and canon.

l4.title: Theology Proper
l4.desc: The attributes of God: omniscience, omnipotence, omnipresence.

l5.title: The Trinity
l5.desc: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit: one God in three persons.

l6.title: Christology I
l6.desc: The person of Christ: divine and human nature.

l7.title: Christology II
l7.desc: The work of Christ: life, death, resurrection, and ascension.

l8.title: Pneumatology
l8.desc: The doctrine of the Holy Spirit: person, work, and gifts.

l9.title: Biblical Anthropology
l9.desc: The doctrine of man: creation, fall, and image of God.

l10.title: Hamartiology
l10.desc: The doctrine of sin: origin, nature, and consequences.

l11.title: Soteriology
l11.desc: The doctrine of salvation: grace, faith, justification, and sanctification.

l12.title: Ecclesiology
l12.desc: The doctrine of the Church: nature, mission, and government.

l13.title: Sacraments
l13.desc: Baptism and Lord's Supper: meaning and practice.

l15.title: Eschatology I
l15.desc: End times: signs, tribulation, and rapture.

l16.title: Eschatology II
l16.desc: Millennium, final judgment, and new heavens and new earth.

l17.title: Hermeneutics
l17.desc: How to interpret the Bible correctly: methods and principles.

l18.title: Church History
l18.desc: From Acts to today: reformations, revivals, and movements.

l19.title: Apologetics
l19.desc: Defending the faith with reason: answers to big questions.

l20.title: Practical Theology
l20.desc: Applying theology in daily life, ministry, and missions.
